title: tax — compliance evidence
description: Tax is FINANCIAL-RECORD-CRITICAL. Every calculation is a record subject to regulator audit for 7-10 years (VAT MOSS, US Wayfair, EU VAT Directive, Canadian GST/HST). Exemption certificates are legal documents. PCI-DSS scope (never card data) applies. GDPR Art. 6(1)(c) legal-obligation basis for retention.

controls.regime: EU VAT MOSS (Mini One Stop Shop)
controls.principle: quarterly VAT filing for digital services across EU member states
controls.evidence: tax_calculations rows include the customer's jurisdiction + rate applied + source_amount + tax_amount. The annual/quarterly report endpoint aggregates by jurisdiction for MOSS filing. Rate snapshots are IMMUTABLE so filed amounts always match issued invoices.

controls.principle: economic nexus tracking for US sales tax
controls.evidence: tax_jurisdictions.nexus_type supports 'physical', 'economic', 'marketplace' — tenants declare their nexus per state. The provider integration (TaxJar / Avalara) reads nexus_addresses on each call. Every calculation snapshots the applicable nexus in provider_metadata for regulator audit.

tax_rates supports multiple concurrent rates per jurisdiction (federal GST 5% + provincial e.g. Ontario HST 8% aggregates to 13% HST). rate_type='gst' | 'hst'. Effective-range time bounds handle provincial fiscal-year rate changes.

Every EU jurisdiction is seeded in the platform reference set with its current standard VAT rate. Effective-range time bounds handle rate changes at fiscal-year boundaries (e.g. UK 20% → 17.5% during COVID, then back to 20%).

controls.principle: legal basis: compliance with legal obligation
controls.evidence: Tax data retention is required by tax authorities (7 years US IRS, 10 years EU under certain regimes). GDPR Art. 17(3)(b) carve-out permits retention beyond a data subject erasure request when necessary to comply with a legal obligation. tax_calculations survive UserErased with subject-identifying fields nulled; the calculation record itself persists for regulator audit.

Certificate documents accessed via time-limited S3 signed URLs, never distributed as base64 blobs in API responses. certificate_number is CONFIDENTIAL — support role sees redacted view. provider credentials + certificate_document_url encrypted at rest.

Customer subject-of-data export includes tax_calculations where subject matches. The export shows applied rate + exemption + calculated tax at each event; certificate documents are exported via 24h-signed S3 URLs (not the underlying encryption keys).

TenantErased cascades hard-delete of tax_rates + tax_jurisdictions + tax_exemptions via FK CASCADE. tax_calculations MIGRATE to a compliance archive linked to invoice retention rather than hard-delete (regulator audit may need them years after the tenant's erasure event). UserErased nulls customer-identifying fields on tax_exemptions while preserving the calculation record.

TaxJar / Avalara / Stripe Tax are Data Processors under Art. 28 — tenant admin acknowledges DPA references before configuring the provider. subprocessors.json documents each. NativeCalculator + CustomWebhook are not Academorix processors (local computation / tenant-hosted respectively).

tax_provider_configs.config (credentials) is encrypted at rest. certificate_document_url (exemption certificate location) is encrypted at rest. Access restricted to owner + admin + finance roles. Support role sees redacted view.

All provider endpoints use TLS 1.2+. Guzzle enforces certificate validation. HMAC signing for CustomWebhook uses SHA256. Certificate documents accessed via time-limited S3 signed URLs (default 15min expiry, max 4h).

TaxProviderCircuitBreakerOpened + TaxCalculationFailed + TaxCalculationDriftDetected are P1 signals. Every fire pages finance ops. academorix.tax.calculations.failed_total + academorix.tax.calculations.drift_detected_total counters tracked; spikes surface a compliance-relevant tax pipeline degradation.
The ReconcileProviderCalculationsJob spot-checks 1% of calculations weekly against provider re-computation. Divergence triggers TaxCalculationDriftDetected + human review before regulator filing.

Calculation-freeze pattern (§4 readme) + fail-safe provider policy (no silent fallback) + immutable audit trail on tax_calculations = defense-in-depth against the compliance-critical failure modes (retroactive rate changes, silent under-charging, missing regulator audit trail).

tax_calculations.source_amount_cents is a currency amount only. NO PAN / CVV / expiry / cardholder name on any tax row. Refund tax reversal carries only the negative amount (no card_last4). Payment references upstream `transaction` module tokens (Wave 4 peer).

Every write to tax_rates / tax_jurisdictions / tax_exemptions / tax_calculations emits an audit row (Auditable trait). Financial-record retention: 7 years (10 for Enterprise via tax_extended_retention entitlement). tax_calculations are immutable after commit — no updates to audit.
Same as GDPR Art. 15 access. Users can request the full tax calculation history where they are the customer.

CCPA §1798.105(d)(6) provides an explicit carve-out for records required to comply with a legal obligation. Tax records survive user delete request; the underlying user data is redacted while the calculation record persists.
